problems.title: Poker Hands I
problems.description: A poker hand consists of five cards from a standard deck of 52 cards. If p is the probability that you have a four-of-a-kind (four of the five cards have the same rank), find the reciprocal of p.

problems.title: River Length
problems.description: You are in a river of unknown length and velocity. You know that if you are stationary on the river, then it takes you 6 seconds for you to reach the other end. If you swim down the river at a rate of 3 feet per second, then it takes you 4 seconds to reach the other end. Find the length of the river (in feet).

problems.title: Coin on Chess Board
problems.description: A chess board consists of 2 inch by 2 inch squares. We toss a coin (diameter of 1 inch) that lands somewhere on the board randomly. What is the probability that the coin is completely within one of the 2 inch by 2 inch squares (not on more than 1 square)?

problems.title: Arithmetic Mean
problems.description: The arithmetic mean of 5, 6, 11, x, and y is 20. What is the arithmetic mean of x and y?

problems.title: St. Petersburg Paradox
problems.description: Suppose you are offered to play a game where you flip a fair coin until you obtain a heads for the first time. If the first heads occurs on the nth flip, you are paid out $2^n. What is the fair value of this game? If your answer is infinite, enter -1.
